commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are compatibility, provider/privacy policy, and live proof decisions rather than only a narrow mechanical repair.; Needs attention: The main security concern is automatic browser screenshot routing to external image-understanding providers without an explicit browser-specific policy decision.; Review finding: [P1] Gate screenshots before external image rou... (sha=64ed5dcf3b0d3d33d48631b9f5c1744cd341d693)
commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors browser screenshot vision through shared media understanding and changes browser/tool/streaming/CLI/media delivery paths toward structured media fields instead of text `MEDIA:` directives.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers from source inspection: current main prints `MEDIA:` for CLI helpers while PR head prints plain paths, and PR head calls media understanding from browser screenshots without carrying `agentId`. I did not run live browser/provider proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are compatibility, provider/privacy policy, and live proof decisions rather than only a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: Refactor browser screenshot vision through shared media understanding
target.body:
## Summary

- Reworks browser screenshot vision to use the existing media image-understanding runtime instead of adding browser-specific model/config plumbing.
- Browser screenshots now ask the shared media-understanding path for a text description when an existing image model is available through media config, agent defaults, or provider auth; otherwise they keep returning the original screenshot image result.
- Removes `MEDIA:` text directives from tool-result, browser, streaming chunk, CLI, QA, QQ context, and message-action delivery paths in favor of structured media fields.
- Keeps legacy `MEDIA:` parsing only at final/public outbound compatibility boundaries, plus SDK media-load input tolerance, and defangs untrusted browser/TTS transcript text before it can reach that boundary.
- Updates docs and tests to describe structured media delivery (`details.media`, `mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`, attachment/path aliases) as the normal path.

## Behavior / User-visible changes

- No new browser config is required: if an image-understanding model is configured, browser screenshot descriptions can use it automatically.
- Text-only main models can reason over screenshot descriptions; image-capable main models or unconfigured installs keep the old image-result fallback.
- Tool outputs and browser vision descriptions no longer synthesize deliverable media from line-start text directives.
- Message/tool callers can send media through structured fields instead of embedding directive text.
- Existing final assistant replies and public outbound/gateway payloads that still use legacy `MEDIA:` lines remain compatible.

## Security / Policy

- Browser vision text is still treated as untrusted page/model output: it is wrapped as external content and line-start `MEDIA:` directives are neutralized.
- Tool-result text no longer feeds the media extractor; trusted media delivery now comes from structured result metadata or explicit media fields.
- TTS transcript text is sanitized so spoken content cannot inject final-reply directives if rendered later.
- The shared media scope policy receives browser runtime context including session key, channel, workspace, and best-effort chat type.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/payloads.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/utils/attachment-tags.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/group/history.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/ref/format-ref-entry.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/decode-media-path.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-runtime-agent-media.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts`
  - 9 Vitest shards passed in 166.64s
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`
  - serial rerun passed after one local post-shard allocator abort
- `autoreview --mode local`
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=0 pnpm check:changed`
  - guard checks passed through dependency/package checks
  - blocked in core typecheck by pre-existing unrelated `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts:844` and `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts:891` errors (`number | undefined` passed where `number` is required)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Browser screenshot vision now works through the shared image-understanding runtime, while media delivery is moved away from tool/browser text directives and into structured media fields.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with focused Vitest coverage for browser vision, tool-result media extraction, final reply compatibility, outbound delivery, TTS transcript sanitization, QA media proof, QQ context formatting, and SDK media loading.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification commands above.

Evidence after fix: Tests cover configured browser image-understanding success, image fallback, `MEDIA:` neutralization in browser vision text, structured tool media extraction (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`, `path`, `filePath`, attachments), final legacy `MEDIA:` compatibility, streamed audio directive stripping, TTS auto-skip on legacy final media replies, and QA extraction from real generated-media `mediaUrls` arrays.

Observed result after fix: Browser screenshot success returns wrapped text with provider/model attribution; unavailable or failed image understanding returns the original image result. Tool/browser/streaming chunk paths no longer turn text `MEDIA:` lines into attachments, while structured media and final legacy replies still deliver media.

What was not tested: Live external provider/browser run after the final structured-media rewrite; `check:changed` could not complete because of the unrelated auth-profiles type errors listed above.
target.author: scotthuang

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
